params.name: Johann Wilhelm's Statue
params.description:
On Apr 6, 1819 AD, Father Johann Wilhelm of Vienna began training guide dogs.
Johann Wilhelm was a pioneer in blind education, famous for training a dog to lead the way by attaching thin twigs to its collar. This dog is recognized as the world's first guide dog. Johann Wilhelm also founded the Vienna Center for the Blind's dog training program, known as the Vienna Blind Reconstruction Center. He published books detailing his methods for training guide dogs. He believed that a soft rope couldn …
